posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — Makes sense. They don’t have a branch or business in India unlike IB. They don’t have to deal with RBI. But rules apply to residents anyway. The onus of compliance rests on you.

posts.author: Walker
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — @BB789 I just a call from Charles Schwab and they are allowing both margin and cash account and unrestricted access to every tradable instrument for Indian tax resident

posts.author: Walker
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — Yeah they may very soon as US investing is getting very popular among retail traders nowadays

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — No. You need to emigrate to have such freedoms. Not making enough to have such options. Also RBI/Govt may even come down on LRS.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — How so? Couldn’t option buying be interpreted as being “party to a derivative contract that you post collateral with”? But would it definitely fit selling covered call? “All of credit risk” would imply so. Again It’s probably ambiguous on purpose so that government can target anyone for other reasons.

posts.author: Walker
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — if we go by this then options by definition doesn’t fit into this, options are right to buy number of stocks at a certain price at a certain date

posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — Master Direction - Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) (Updated as on September 06, 2024) Master Directions - Reserve Bank of India All other transactions which are otherwise not permissible under FEMA AND those in the nature of remittance for margins or margin calls to overseas exchanges/ overseas counterparty are not allowed under the Scheme. Margin is not exactly defined in the LRS master direction. But somewhere in other directions it says the below. This may or may not be applicable definition: Foreign Exchange Management (Margin for Deriv…

posts.author: Walker
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — As posted in the screenshot under LRS only cash account is allowed no margin account, and buying options doesn’t require any margin, so can it be interpreted that buying options is permitted…?

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — Excerpts from AI: The Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) governs all foreign exchange transactions in India, while the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) is a specific provision under FEMA that allows residents to remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year for certain permissible transactions. Transactions outside the LRS may require prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) For transactions that exceed the LRS limit or are not covered under it, individuals must seek prior approval from the RBI. These may include Direct investmen…

posts.author: Walker

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Hella confused with option trading in US market — The ambiguity is probably on purpose. One possible explanation is it is not permitted under LRS, but permitted outside of it nonetheless.
